Title: Verify Diagnoses widget shows active confirmed diagnoses with correct ordering
Preconditions: Clinician logged in; patient has multiple coded diagnoses recorded across visits
Test_Steps: 1. Open the patient dashboard | 2. Locate the Diagnoses widget | 3. Compare displayed diagnoses against recorded encounter diagnoses
Test_Data: Patient with Dx: Type 2 Diabetes (confirmed, primary), Hypertension (confirmed, secondary)
Expected_Result: Widget lists active confirmed diagnoses; primary/secondary order and certainty match recorded data; no resolved diagnoses shown in active list

Title: Verify Diagnoses widget distinguishes presumed vs confirmed certainty
Preconditions: Patient has diagnoses with mixed certainty values
Test_Steps: 1. Record a presumed diagnosis | 2. Record a confirmed diagnosis | 3. Open dashboard Diagnoses widget
Expected_Result: Each diagnosis displays its certainty indicator correctly; presumed and confirmed are visually distinguishable; no certainty defaulting errors

Title: Verify Diagnoses widget empty state when no diagnoses recorded
Preconditions: Newly registered patient with zero diagnoses
Test_Steps: 1. Open the new patient dashboard | 2. Inspect the Diagnoses widget
Expected_Result: Widget renders an empty-state message (e.g. no diagnoses) without error, blank table, or stale data

Title: Verify drill-down from Diagnoses widget opens the source encounter
Preconditions: Patient has a diagnosis linked to a specific encounter
Test_Steps: 1. Open Diagnoses widget | 2. Click a diagnosis entry | 3. Observe navigation
Expected_Result: Clicking the diagnosis drills into the originating encounter/visit context; encounter date and provider match the diagnosis source

Title: Verify Diagnoses widget hidden for user lacking view privilege
Preconditions: User role without Get Diagnoses / dashboard widget privilege
Test_Steps: 1. Log in as restricted user | 2. Open the patient dashboard | 3. Look for the Diagnoses widget
Test_Data: Role=Registration Clerk without clinical view privilege
Expected_Result: Diagnoses widget is not rendered or shows access-denied; no diagnosis PHI is exposed in DOM or API payload to the restricted user

Title: Verify Diagnoses widget reflects ICD-10/SNOMED coded mapping
Preconditions: Diagnosis recorded with a concept mapped to ICD-10 and SNOMED CT
Test_Steps: 1. Record diagnosis with mapped concept | 2. Retrieve Condition via FHIR R4 | 3. Compare widget label to coded mapping
Test_Data: Dx concept mapped: SNOMED 73211009, ICD-10 E11.9
Expected_Result: FHIR Condition.code contains correct SNOMED/ICD-10 codings with proper system URIs; widget display text matches preferred term

Title: Verify Diagnoses widget loading state during slow backend
Preconditions: Network throttled to simulate slow API response
Test_Steps: 1. Throttle network | 2. Open dashboard | 3. Observe widget state progression
Test_Data: Latency injected 5s on encounter diagnosis endpoint
Expected_Result: Widget transitions Loading -> Loaded; shows spinner/placeholder during fetch; no flash of empty-state before data resolves

Title: Verify Latest Obs widget shows most recent value per concept
Preconditions: Patient has multiple observations for the same concept over time
Test_Steps: 1. Record three serial obs for one concept | 2. Open Latest Obs widget | 3. Verify displayed value
Expected_Result: Widget shows only the latest value (130) with its datetime; older values not shown in latest column; concept label correct

Title: Verify Latest Obs widget handles tie-break on identical obsDatetime
Preconditions: Two observations for the same concept recorded with identical obsDatetime
Test_Steps: 1. Record two obs same concept same timestamp | 2. Open Latest Obs widget
Test_Data: Two Hemoglobin obs at 2026-06-30T10:00:00, values 12.0 and 12.5
Expected_Result: Widget deterministically displays one value using a documented tie-break (e.g. highest obs id); no duplicate rows or crash

Title: Verify Latest Obs widget renders abnormal/critical flags
Preconditions: Numeric concept with reference ranges configured
Test_Steps: 1. Record obs below low-critical, within range, and above high-critical | 2. Open widget
Expected_Result: Each value displays the correct normal/abnormal/critical flag per configured reference range; no mis-flagging at boundaries

Title: Verify Latest Obs widget rejects rendering of voided observations
Preconditions: An observation was recorded then voided
Test_Steps: 1. Record an obs | 2. Void the obs | 3. Open Latest Obs widget
Test_Data: Obs=Temp 39.0 then voided with reason 'entry error'
Expected_Result: Voided observation is excluded; widget shows the prior valid value or empty-state; voided data never surfaced as latest

Title: Verify Latest Obs widget unit display matches concept numeric units
Preconditions: Numeric concepts with configured units
Test_Steps: 1. Record numeric obs | 2. Open widget | 3. Verify unit suffix
Expected_Result: Each value displays the correct unit from concept configuration; no unit omission or mismatch

Title: Verify Latest Obs widget refresh after new obs without full page reload
Preconditions: Dashboard open; clinician records a new obs in another tab/encounter
Test_Steps: 1. Open dashboard | 2. Record new obs elsewhere | 3. Trigger widget refresh
Test_Data: New Glucose=145 recorded after dashboard load
Expected_Result: After refresh the widget shows the new latest value; stale cached value is replaced; refresh does not duplicate rows

Title: Verify Vitals widget shows latest vital set with capture datetime
Preconditions: Patient has at least one vitals encounter
Test_Steps: 1. Open dashboard | 2. Locate Vitals widget | 3. Compare to recorded vitals
Expected_Result: Widget displays latest vitals set with correct values, units and capture datetime matching the encounter

Title: Verify Vitals widget BP boundary flagging at hypertensive thresholds
Preconditions: BP reference thresholds configured
Test_Steps: 1. Record BP at threshold boundaries | 2. Open Vitals widget
Expected_Result: Each BP is classified at the correct boundary (normal/elevated/stage1/stage2/crisis); no off-by-one classification at exact thresholds

Title: Verify Vitals widget rejects physiologically impossible values
Preconditions: Vitals capture form available
Test_Steps: 1. Attempt to enter out-of-range vitals | 2. Save | 3. Observe widget
Expected_Result: Form validation blocks impossible values or widget does not render them as valid; absurd readings never displayed as patient data

Title: Verify Vitals widget partial vital set displays available metrics only
Preconditions: A vitals encounter where only some metrics were recorded
Test_Steps: 1. Record partial vitals | 2. Open widget
Test_Data: Recorded only Pulse=80 and Temp=37.2; BP omitted
Expected_Result: Widget shows captured metrics; missing metrics render as empty/dash, not zero or stale prior values

Title: Verify Vitals widget unit-system handling for imperial vs metric
Preconditions: System supports configurable unit display
Test_Steps: 1. Configure metric | 2. Verify widget | 3. Switch to imperial | 4. Verify widget
Expected_Result: Values convert correctly per selected unit system; no rounding error beyond defined precision; unit labels update consistently

Title: Verify Vitals widget error state on backend obs API failure
Preconditions: Ability to force a 500 on the vitals/obs endpoint
Test_Steps: 1. Stub obs endpoint to 500 | 2. Open dashboard | 3. Observe Vitals widget
Expected_Result: Widget shows a graceful error state with retry affordance; does not display blank-as-normal or partially rendered stale vitals

Title: Verify Recent Visits widget lists visits in reverse chronological order
Preconditions: Patient has multiple visits including active and past
Test_Steps: 1. Open dashboard | 2. Locate Recent Visits widget | 3. Verify order
Expected_Result: Visits listed newest first; active visit indicated; start/stop datetimes and visit type correct

Title: Verify Recent Visits widget active vs ended visit state indicators
Preconditions: Patient with one active visit that can be ended
Test_Steps: 1. Open widget with active visit | 2. End the visit | 3. Refresh widget
Expected_Result: Visit transitions from active to ended; widget updates state indicator and shows stop datetime after refresh

Title: Verify Recent Visits widget drill-down opens visit encounters
Preconditions: A visit containing multiple encounters
Test_Steps: 1. Open Recent Visits widget | 2. Click a visit | 3. Verify encounters shown
Expected_Result: Drill-down displays that visit's encounters with correct types, providers and datetimes matching source data

Title: Verify Recent Visits widget pagination/limit boundary
Preconditions: Patient has more visits than the widget display limit
Test_Steps: 1. Create visits exceeding limit | 2. Open widget | 3. Verify count and view-more
Expected_Result: Widget shows exactly the configured limit with a view-all affordance; no truncation that drops the most recent visit

Title: Verify Recent Visits widget visit location accuracy
Preconditions: Visits recorded at different locations
Test_Steps: 1. Record visits at two locations | 2. Retrieve Encounter via FHIR | 3. Compare to widget
Expected_Result: FHIR Encounter.location references match widget-displayed locations; no location mislabeling

Title: Verify Family History widget displays relationships and conditions
Preconditions: Patient has family history entries recorded
Test_Steps: 1. Record family member relationship and condition | 2. Open Family widget
Expected_Result: Widget shows relationship type, condition and onset detail accurately; no cross-mapping of relation to condition

Title: Verify Family History widget empty state
Preconditions: Patient with no family history recorded
Test_Steps: 1. Open dashboard | 2. Inspect Family widget
Expected_Result: Widget shows clear empty-state; no error and no placeholder rows

Title: Verify Family History widget PHI-safe rendering of relative identifiers
Preconditions: Family entries that could contain free-text names
Test_Steps: 1. Record family entry with free-text note | 2. Open widget | 3. Inspect rendering
Test_Data: Free-text note containing a relative name
Expected_Result: Free-text is rendered safely (HTML-escaped) without script execution; relative PHI shown only to authorized roles

Title: Verify Conditions widget separates active and inactive conditions
Preconditions: Patient has active and resolved conditions
Test_Steps: 1. Record an active condition | 2. Record then resolve another | 3. Open Conditions widget
Expected_Result: Active conditions listed in active section; resolved conditions shown as inactive/resolved with end date; no overlap

Title: Verify Conditions widget clinicalStatus state transition active to resolved
Preconditions: An active condition that can be resolved
Test_Steps: 1. Open widget with active condition | 2. Resolve the condition | 3. Refresh widget
Expected_Result: Condition moves from active to inactive section; clinicalStatus and end date update; transition is auditable

Title: Verify Conditions widget FHIR Condition mapping of clinical and verification status
Preconditions: Conditions with various statuses recorded
Test_Steps: 1. Record condition | 2. Retrieve FHIR Condition R4 | 3. Compare to widget
Expected_Result: FHIR clinicalStatus and verificationStatus use correct code system URIs and match widget display; no status defaulting

Title: Verify Conditions widget non-coded free-text condition handling
Preconditions: Condition recorded as non-coded free text
Test_Steps: 1. Record a non-coded condition | 2. Open widget
Expected_Result: Free-text condition displays with its text; flagged/handled as non-coded; not silently dropped or mis-coded

Title: Verify Allergies widget shows allergen, reaction and severity
Preconditions: Patient has allergies recorded
Test_Steps: 1. Open dashboard | 2. Locate Allergies widget | 3. Verify entries
Expected_Result: Widget shows allergen, reaction(s) and severity correctly; severe allergies are visually prominent

Title: Verify Allergies widget renders Unknown vs No Known Allergies distinctly
Preconditions: Patients with each allergy assertion state
Test_Steps: 1. Patient A no allergy data | 2. Patient B marked No Known Allergies | 3. Compare widgets
Expected_Result: Unassessed (unknown) and explicit No Known Allergies are displayed distinctly; never conflate absence of data with confirmed NKA

Title: Verify Allergies widget multiple reactions per allergen
Preconditions: Allergen with multiple reactions recorded
Test_Steps: 1. Record allergen with multiple reactions | 2. Open widget
Expected_Result: All reactions are listed for the allergen; none dropped or merged incorrectly

Title: Verify Allergies widget critical-allergy visibility cannot be permission-suppressed silently
Preconditions: Role with limited clinical view
Test_Steps: 1. Log in as limited role | 2. Open dashboard | 3. Inspect Allergies widget behavior
Test_Data: Role lacking allergy view; patient with severe Penicillin allergy
Expected_Result: Either allergies are shown for safety or an explicit access-restricted notice appears; widget never renders silently empty implying NKA

Title: Verify Allergies widget FHIR AllergyIntolerance criticality mapping
Preconditions: Allergies recorded with severity
Test_Steps: 1. Record severe allergy | 2. Retrieve FHIR AllergyIntolerance | 3. Compare
Expected_Result: FHIR AllergyIntolerance.criticality and reaction.severity map correctly; coding systems valid; widget label consistent

Title: Verify Allergies widget HL7 ADT AL1 segment ingestion reflects on dashboard
Preconditions: Inbound HL7 ADT with allergy AL1 segments
Test_Steps: 1. Send ADT^A01 with AL1 allergy | 2. Process message | 3. Open Allergies widget
Expected_Result: Allergy from AL1 segment is ingested and displayed with correct allergen, severity and reaction; malformed AL1 is rejected/logged

Title: Verify Attachments widget lists uploaded documents with metadata
Preconditions: Patient has uploaded attachments
Test_Steps: 1. Upload a document | 2. Open Attachments widget
Test_Data: File=labreport.pdf; type=Lab Report; uploaded by clinician
Expected_Result: Widget lists attachment with filename, type, uploader and datetime; thumbnail/preview affordance present

Title: Verify Attachments widget rejects disallowed file types and oversize files
Preconditions: Attachment upload available with configured limits
Test_Steps: 1. Attempt upload of disallowed type | 2. Attempt oversize upload | 3. Observe widget
Expected_Result: Disallowed and oversize uploads are rejected with clear error; rejected files never appear in widget; no partial corrupt entry

Title: Verify Attachments preview enforces authorization on document retrieval
Preconditions: Two patients with attachments; restricted user
Test_Steps: 1. Capture attachment URL of patient A | 2. As unauthorized user request that URL | 3. Observe
Test_Data: Direct attachment object URL for patient A
Expected_Result: Direct object access is authorized server-side; unauthorized retrieval returns 403/redirect; no IDOR exposing PHI documents

Title: Verify Attachments widget handles corrupt/unrenderable file gracefully
Preconditions: An attachment record whose binary is corrupt/missing
Test_Steps: 1. Reference a corrupt attachment | 2. Open widget | 3. Attempt preview
Expected_Result: Widget lists the entry but preview shows a graceful fallback (cannot render); no crash of the dashboard or other widgets

Title: Verify Attachments upload audit trail recorded
Preconditions: Audit logging enabled
Test_Steps: 1. Upload attachment | 2. Query audit log
Expected_Result: Audit entry records who uploaded, when, patient, filename and action; entry is tamper-evident and queryable

Title: Verify Weight graph plots chronological weight observations accurately
Preconditions: Patient has several weight observations
Test_Steps: 1. Record serial weights | 2. Open Weight graph widget | 3. Verify plotted points
Expected_Result: Graph plots points at correct dates and values; axis scaling correct; trend line direction reflects data

Title: Verify Weight graph single-point and empty data rendering
Preconditions: Patients with zero and exactly one weight obs
Test_Steps: 1. Patient A no weight | 2. Patient B one weight | 3. Open Weight graph each
Expected_Result: Empty data shows graceful empty-state; single point renders without broken axis or line-of-one error

Title: Verify Weight graph excludes voided weight observations
Preconditions: A weight obs that was voided
Test_Steps: 1. Record weights | 2. Void one | 3. Open Weight graph
Expected_Result: Voided weight is not plotted; graph reflects only valid observations; trend recalculated correctly

Title: Verify Weight graph unit consistency across mixed-unit observations
Preconditions: Weight obs stored consistently in base unit
Test_Steps: 1. Query obs values in DB | 2. Compare to graph plotted values
Test_Data: DB weight obs stored in kg
Expected_Result: All plotted values derive from a single normalized unit; no kg/lb mixing causing spurious spikes

Title: Verify Appointments widget shows upcoming appointments with status
Preconditions: Patient has scheduled appointments
Test_Steps: 1. Open dashboard | 2. Locate Appointments widget | 3. Verify entries
Expected_Result: Upcoming appointments listed with date, time, provider, service and status; ordered by soonest first

Title: Verify Appointments widget status transitions Scheduled to CheckedIn to Completed
Preconditions: An appointment that can move through statuses
Test_Steps: 1. Open widget Scheduled | 2. Check in | 3. Complete | 4. Refresh each step
Expected_Result: Widget reflects each status transition after refresh; invalid transitions blocked; status history consistent

Title: Verify Appointments widget cancelled and missed appointment handling
Preconditions: Appointments in cancelled and missed states
Test_Steps: 1. Cancel one appointment | 2. Mark another missed | 3. Open widget
Expected_Result: Cancelled shows reason and is visually distinct; missed flagged appropriately; neither counted as upcoming active

Title: Verify Appointments widget timezone/DST correctness of displayed times
Preconditions: Appointment scheduled across a DST boundary
Test_Steps: 1. Schedule appt near DST change | 2. View widget in configured timezone
Test_Data: Appt at 2026-11-01 01:30 local across DST fallback
Expected_Result: Displayed local time matches scheduled instant accounting for DST; no off-by-one-hour or duplicated-hour error

Title: Verify Appointments widget no-cross-patient leakage via API filter
Preconditions: Two patients with appointments
Test_Steps: 1. Call appointments API for patient A | 2. Inspect returned payload
Expected_Result: Only patient A appointments returned; patient B data never present; tampering patient param does not bypass authorization

Title: Verify dashboard partial-failure isolation between widgets
Preconditions: Ability to fail one widget endpoint only
Test_Steps: 1. Fail the Diagnoses endpoint | 2. Load dashboard | 3. Inspect other widgets
Expected_Result: Failed widget shows error state in isolation; other widgets render normally; one failure does not blank the whole dashboard

Title: Verify dashboard widget refresh-all updates all widgets consistently
Preconditions: Data changed in backend after initial load
Test_Steps: 1. Load dashboard | 2. Change data backend | 3. Trigger refresh-all
Expected_Result: All widgets refetch and show updated data; no widget retains stale cache; refresh completes without partial states

Title: Verify widget visibility configuration per role (pairwise)
Preconditions: Configurable widget visibility by role and privilege
Test_Steps: 1. Configure widget set for each role | 2. Log in per role | 3. Verify visible widgets
Test_Data: Roles x widgets: Clinician/all, Nurse/vitals+allergies, Clerk/none-clinical
Expected_Result: Each role sees exactly its permitted widget set; no privileged widget leaks to lower role; configuration honored consistently

Title: Verify dashboard widgets accessibility for keyboard and screen reader
Preconditions: Assistive tech available (screen reader, keyboard only)
Test_Steps: 1. Tab through each widget | 2. Activate drill-downs via keyboard | 3. Run screen reader
Expected_Result: All widgets are reachable and operable by keyboard; ARIA roles/labels announce widget titles and data; focus order logical; WCAG 2.1 AA met

Title: Verify dashboard widget data access is audit-logged
Preconditions: Audit logging enabled for record access
Test_Steps: 1. Open patient dashboard | 2. Query audit log for access events
Expected_Result: Access to patient clinical data via widgets is audit-logged with user, patient, timestamp and accessed resource per HIPAA

Title: Verify dashboard exploratory probing of widget loading race conditions
Preconditions: Slow and fast endpoints mixed; rapid patient switching
Test_Steps: 1. Open dashboard | 2. Rapidly switch patients before widgets finish loading | 3. Observe
Test_Data: Switch between patient A and B repeatedly during load
Expected_Result: No widget shows patient A data under patient B context; late responses are discarded; no PHI cross-contamination from stale requests

Title: Verify Latest Obs widget XSS-safe rendering of free-text obs values
Preconditions: Concept supporting free-text obs values
Test_Steps: 1. Record obs with script payload as text value | 2. Open Latest Obs widget
Expected_Result: Payload is rendered as inert escaped text; no script executes; widget and dashboard remain intact

Title: Verify Diagnoses widget concurrent edit reflects latest after refresh
Preconditions: Two clinicians editing diagnoses concurrently
Test_Steps: 1. Clinician A views widget | 2. Clinician B adds diagnosis | 3. A refreshes
Test_Data: B adds Dx=Anemia while A viewing
Expected_Result: After refresh A sees B's diagnosis; no lost update; widget reflects authoritative server state

Title: Verify Vitals widget BMI derivation consistency from weight and height
Preconditions: Patient with recorded weight and height
Test_Steps: 1. Record weight and height | 2. View Vitals/derived widget | 3. Recompute BMI
Expected_Result: Displayed BMI equals weight/(height_m^2) within rounding precision; no stale BMI when weight/height change
